senses.definition: to do a task in the most complete way

examples.x: The Siemens display goes the whole hog.
We only planned to order appetizers, but we went whole hog and ended up getting a seven-course meal.

senses.definition: (of a task, event, situation, etc.) completely finished or taken care of

examples.x: After scoring the winning goal, the team celebrated their victory, knowing that the game was done and dusted.
We completed all the tasks on the project checklist, so it's finally done and dusted.
After weeks of studying, I finished the last question and handed in my paper feeling relieved that the exam was done and dusted.
The final touches have been added, and the remodeling project is now done and dusted.
The judge reviewed the evidence and made the final ruling, declaring the case done and dusted.
We visited all the places on our itinerary, and now the vacation is done and dusted.
The negotiations were tough, but we finally got the deal done and dusted.
With the final edits and proofreading complete, the manuscript is done and dusted.

senses.definition: having been formally authenticated by using signatures and official seals or stamps

examples.x: Well, the agreement is signed and sealed, and there's nothing you can do or say about it.

word_list.pronunciation: f…Ļ Ćm sňąuňźp t…ô nňą Ćts

senses.definition: from beginning to end, including all the steps or components of a process or event

examples.x: The whole building, soup to nuts, cost less to build than that parking lot.
This is how you do a film campaign from soup to nuts.

senses.definition: the part of a race, competition, etc. that is considered its last

examples.x: Clinton also had two personal strikes against him as he went into the home stretch toward the July Democratic convention.
We‚Äôve been on this project for three months, but we‚Äôre in the home stretch now.

senses.definition: used to refer to something one is dealing with

examples.x: Work is in hand on identifying information needs.
Don't worry, I have the preparations for the party well in hand - everything will be taken care of.
I know work has been piling up while I've been away, but the project you're so concerned about is in hand.
The project is in hand, and we are making good progress towards meeting the deadline.
The lawyer assured the client that the legal case was in hand and that they were working diligently to resolve it.
